title: Vydel Dir'Nul
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Dark Jedi |image=[[File:Vydel.jpg]] |name=Vydel Dir'Nul
Kardem |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]{{Tales|17|Dark Journey (comic story)}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Black]] |feathers= |eyes=Black |skin=Light |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners=[[Ash B'risko]] |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] {{C|as Dir'Nul}} *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] {{C|as Dir'Nul}} *[[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] {{C|as "Kardem"}} |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|I know no Vydel Dir'Nul…she must be ''dead''. But since you want to find her so badly, perhaps you should JOIN her!|Vydel Dir'Nul, under the personality of the Dark Jedi Kardem|}} '''Vydel Dir'Nul''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who lived during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. One [[Standard month/Legends|month]] before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|initiation]] of the Clone Wars, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] assigned Dir'Nul to serve as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] for an individual. During her mission, Dir'Nul fell in love with the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Ash B'risko]] and entered into a romantic relationship with him. However, she later caught B'risko in the arms of a [[Unidentified Twi'lek female (Kardem's first Twi'lek victim)|Twi'lek female]]. As she [[Death/Legends|slew]] B'risko and the woman, Dir'Nul created the personality of the male [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] '''Kardem''', whom she believed was a different individual. After the murders, Dir'Nul was unaware of her [[Multiple personality disorder|split personality]] or that she was responsible for the murder of B'risko. When the Clone Wars began, Dir'Nul refused to become a [[Jedi General/Legends|general]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]], instead devoting her resources to tracking Kardem. After traveling to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Perilious]], she killed three more [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]], acting as Kardem. Six months later, as Dir'Nul investigated their murders, she was confronted by a [[Unidentified Jedi (Kardem's victim)|male Jedi]], whom she assumed was Kardem. After the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] defeated her in a [[Duel on Perilious|lightsaber duel]], Dir'Nul changed to the Kardem personality and attacked him with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. The Jedi explained that the Council had sent him after Dir'Nul, to bring her in for her crimes dead or alive and then attempted to kill her. However, she intercepted the attack and killed the Jedi. After his death, Dir'Nul reverted to her original personality, and believed him to another victim of Kardem. Eventually, a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was placed on her alter ego of Kardem, and she was captured by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Feskitt Bobb]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Dark. Me.|Dir'Nul, under the Kardem personality|}} [[File:AshBrisko-Tales17.jpg|thumb|left|Ash B'risko, the man with whom Dir'Nul fell in love]] Vydel Dir'Nul, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served during the final [[Time/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]], Dir'Nul was assigned by the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] to serve as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] for an individual. During the mission, she fell in love with a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] named [[Ash B'risko]]. She entered a romantic relationship with the pilot, going against the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. However, she eventually discovered B'risko in the arms of a [[Unidentified Twi'lek female (Kardem's first Twi'lek victim)|female Twi'lek]] dancer. Dir'Nul [[Death/Legends|murdered]] both B'risko and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]], and in doing so, [[Multiple personality disorder|created the personality]] of a male [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] named Kardem, whom she believed to be a different individual. As Kardem she began to murder female Twi'leks while, in her original personality, she began tracking "him" to avenge his victims, especially Ash. {{CSWECite|I|190|Dir'Nul, Vydel}} After the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]—the Republic's conflict with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]—began at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]], Dir'Nul refused to return to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and become a [[Jedi General/Legends|general]] in the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Republic Army]]. Dir'Nul buried B'risko and began tracking Kardem in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]], killing two more Twi'lek females while acting as the Dark Jedi. By the time she arrived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Perilious]]—which was known for its Twi'lek [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] tradings—Dir'Nul had slain three more Twi'lek females as Kardem. [[File:DirNulDuel.jpg|thumb|right|175px|Dir'Nul duels a Jedi sent by the Council to apprehend her on Perilious.]] Six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, while Dir'Nul was investigating the murders of the three Twi'leks, a [[Unidentified Jedi (Kardem's victim)|robed man]] with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] revealed himself to her, and she attacked him, assuming he was Kardem. After a brief [[Duel on Perilious|lightsaber duel]], the man gained the upper hand and defeated Dir'Nul. As he informed her that he was going to bring her back to the Jedi Council for her crimes, Dir'Nul reverted to the Kardem personality—who was unaware that he was Dir'Nul—and unleashed bolts of [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] against her attacker. After the Jedi used Dir'Nul's name while pleading with her to stop attacking him, Kardem told the man he was going to kill him. The man revealed that he was a Jedi and that he had been tracking Dir'Nul for six months. The Jedi ignited his lightsaber and used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|throw]] it at Dir'Nul, determined to bring her to the Council dead or alive. However, Dir'Nul, who was still mentally Kardem, used the Force to stop the lightsaber midflight and attacked the Jedi with more lightning. As Kardem was [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] the Jedi, he claimed that he did not know Dir'Nul and threw the lightsaber back at the Jedi, [[Sai tok/Legends|cutting him in half]]. Dir'Nul reverted to her original personality following her attacker's death and was shocked to learn that Kardem had killed a Jedi. She vowed to stop Kardem no matter what it took, unaware that she was the one who had actually committed the crime. A [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] was eventually placed on Dir'Nul's split personality of Kardem. The serial killer was captured by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Feskitt Bobb]].{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} ==Personality and traits== The Human female Vydel Dir'Nul served the Jedi Order as a Jedi Knight. During her mission to protect an individual, she fell in love with the pilot Ash B'risko, willingly going against the Jedi Code. When she discovered B'risko in a Twi'lek dancer's arms, Dir'Nul was overcome with anger, and she killed both of them immediately. During the act of murdering B'risko and the Twi'lek, Dir'Nul unknowingly created an [[Multiple personality disorder|alter ego]] of a male Dark Jedi named Kardem. After burying B'risko, she desired answers and vengeance, and dedicated herself to capturing Kardem. [[File:Kardem.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Dir'Nul, under the personality of the Dark Jedi Kardem]] Following the initiation of the Clone Wars, Dir'Nul was not interested in fighting for the Republic's [[Politics/Legends|politicians]], whom she viewed as greedy and self-serving, and so refused to serve as a Jedi General. Neither Dir'Nul nor Kardem was aware of the other's existence. During her search for Kardem, she continued her vendetta against Twi'lek females, violently murdering them as Kardem. After she reverted to her normal personality, Dir'Nul would hear Kardem's voice, which would degrade Twi'leks and taunt her. After she was defeated in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] by the male Jedi, she adopted the Kardem personality and began torturing him. When Kardem killed the Jedi, Dir'Nul reverted to her original personality and vowed to stop Kardem, regardless of how long it took and no matter what she had to sacrifice. ==Powers and abilities== Dir'Nul possessed the ability to move objects with the Force; she used this ability against the male Jedi sent by the Council to apprehend her. When Dir'Nul switched her personality to that of the serial killer Kardem, she possessed [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark-side]] [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]], including Force lightning. She used this power against numerous Twi'leks and against the male Jedi. However, when Dir'Nul dueled the Jedi sent to apprehend her, he was able to best her, and he noted that she had let her skills at lightsaber combat deteriorate. After the Jedi threw his lightsaber at her, she was able to stop it midflight. Dir'Nul herself wielded a lightsaber containing an [[orange]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. ==Behind the scenes== Vydel Dir'Nul and her split personality of Kardem first appeared in the short [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] "[[Dark Journey (comic story)|Dark Journey]]," which was featured in [[Star Wars Tales 17|''Star Wars Tales'' 17]] and written by [[Jason Hall]]. In the comic, she was drawn by [[Ben Templesmith]]. The story was later compiled into a trade paperback version and republished in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 5]]''. Plausible stories in [[Star Wars Tales 1|''Star Wars Tales'' 1]]–''[[Star Wars Tales 20|20]]'' were deemed [[Canon|S-canon]] until referenced in a non-''Tales'' source, at which point the subject matter was elevated to C-canon status.{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=752#14952461|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-06-04|time=3:05 AM}} Dir'Nul was first referenced outside ''Tales'' in the article [[The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors|''The History of the Mandalorians'']], written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and published in [[Star Wars Insider 80|''Star Wars Insider'' 80]]. Her split personality of Kardem was used as part of the background for the Mandalorian Feskitt Bobb. Her appearance in ''The History of the Mandalorians'' elevated ''Dark Journey'' to C-canon. Dir'Nul later received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *{{Tales|17|Dark Journey (comic story)}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Vydel Dir'Nul}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Vydel Dir'Nul }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Dir'Nul, Vydel}} [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Knights]]
